common.footerDesc: Free browser puzzles. No download. No account needed.
common.watchAdHint: Watch a short ad to reveal a hint?
common.watchAdUndo: Watch ad to undo last move?
common.watchAdLife: Watch ad for an extra life?

landing.title: PuzzleVault — Free Online Puzzle Games
landing.metaDesc: Play 10 free brain puzzle games directly in your browser. No download, no account needed. Daily challenges, score tracking, and more.
landing.hero: Free Brain Puzzles — No Download Needed
landing.heroSub: Challenge your mind with 10 unique puzzle games. Play directly in your browser.
landing.dailySub: Fresh puzzles every day — can you keep your streak alive?
landing.statsSub: Track your progress across all games

numvault.metaDesc: Can you crack the hidden number code? NumVault is a free number deduction puzzle with daily challenges.
numvault.guide: NumVault is a number deduction puzzle inspired by classic code-breaking games. Your goal is to guess the hidden number code within a limited number of attempts. After each guess, you receive color-coded feedback for every digit: green means the digit is correct and in the right position, yellow means the digit exists in the code but is in the wrong spot, and dark means the digit is not in the code at all. Use the Number Tracker to keep track of which digits have been confirmed, included, or eliminated. Start with common digits and use process of elimination. In Speed Mode, race against the clock to solve as many codes as possible. Each correct answer earns bonus time. Can you crack the vault?
numvault.faq1a: Green = correct digit in correct position. Yellow = digit exists but in wrong position. Dark = digit not in the code. Use these clues to narrow down the answer.
numvault.faq2a: The Number Tracker displays the status of each digit (0-9) below the grid. It shows whether each digit has been confirmed, is possibly included, has been eliminated, or hasn't been used yet.
numvault.faq3a: Speed Mode gives you 60 seconds to solve as many codes as possible. Each correct answer adds 15 seconds to your timer.
numvault.faq4q: How is the Daily Challenge different?
numvault.faq4a: Everyone gets the same puzzle each day. Your results show a streak count and can be shared with friends.
numvault.faq5a: Yes! Tap the hint button to watch a short reward ad and reveal one digit's correct position. Limited to 1 per Daily game and 2 per Free Play game.

gridsmash.tagline: Place blocks, clear lines, chase combos
gridsmash.metaDesc: GridSmash is a free block placement puzzle. Clear lines, trigger combos, and use special blocks for massive scores.
gridsmash.guide: GridSmash is a strategic block placement puzzle. Each turn, you receive three block pieces to place on a 10×10 grid. Drag and drop pieces into valid positions. When an entire row or column is filled, it clears and earns points. Clearing multiple lines at once triggers combos for bonus points. Watch for special blocks: Crystal blocks clear a 3×3 area, Frost blocks freeze cells that require two clears to remove, and Wild blocks fill any single gap. Every 10 turns, the Shatter Zone activates on the bottom rows — clear lines there for triple points, but hurry before the zone expires and fills with random blocks!
gridsmash.faq1a: Crystal (💎) clears a 3×3 area around it. Frost (🧊) freezes 2 random cells that take 2 line clears to remove. Wild (⭐) is a 1×1 block you can place on any empty cell.
gridsmash.faq2a: Every 10 turns, the bottom 2 rows glow gold. Line clears in this zone earn 3× points. The zone lasts 5 turns — if you don't clear it, those rows partially fill with random blocks.
gridsmash.faq3a: The game ends when you receive 3 new pieces but none of them can fit anywhere on the grid.
gridsmash.faq4a: Zen Mode removes the timer, score, and Shatter Zone. It's a relaxed way to play without any pressure — perfect for unwinding.
gridsmash.faq5a: Classic: play until game over. Daily Challenge: same piece sequence for everyone with a 3-minute timer. Zen: no score or pressure, just relax. Sprint 40: race to clear 40 lines as fast as possible.

patternpop.metaDesc: Test your visual memory with PatternPop. Memorize flashing patterns and tap them back. Beware of decoy flashes!
patternpop.guide: PatternPop is a visual memory challenge. Each round, several cells on the grid briefly light up. Memorize their positions, then tap them from memory. Get them all right to advance to the next round with more cells to remember. You have 3 lives — each wrong tap costs one. Starting from Round 10, decoy flashes appear briefly alongside the real pattern. Decoys are lighter in color and shorter in duration. Stay focused to tell real from fake. How many rounds can you survive?
patternpop.faq1a: From Round 10, extra cells flash briefly (0.3 seconds) in a lighter color alongside the real targets. They're designed to trick you — ignore them and focus on the cells that stay lit longer.
patternpop.faq2q: How do I get more lives?
patternpop.faq2a: You start with 3 lives. When you lose all of them, you can watch a short ad to get 1 extra life and continue from the current round.
patternpop.faq3a: Score increases with the number of cells memorized and the round number. You earn a bonus for completing a round without losing lives, plus extra points for ignoring decoys.
patternpop.faq4a: Use the 💡 Hint button to replay the pattern at 50% slower speed. Your first hint each session is free; subsequent hints require watching a reward ad.
patternpop.faq5q: What's the difference between Daily and Endless modes?
patternpop.faq5a: Classic: 3 lives, increasing difficulty. Daily: seeded sequence everyone shares, one attempt per day. Endless: no lives limit, see how far you can go. Speed: tiles flash faster each round.

sortstack.metaDesc: SortStack is a free color sorting puzzle. Move blocks between stacks to group colors. Can you solve it in minimum moves?
sortstack.guide: SortStack is a color sorting puzzle. You see several stacks (drawers) filled with colored blocks. Your goal is to sort them so each stack contains only one color. You can only move the top block from any stack, and it can only be placed on the same color or an empty stack. When a stack is complete (4 matching blocks), it locks and a new empty stack appears. In Hard and Expert modes, you have a limited number of moves. Plan your moves carefully! Unlimited undo is available.
sortstack.faq1a: When you complete a stack of 4 matching colored blocks, it locks with a sparkle effect and a new empty stack appears, giving you more workspace.
sortstack.faq2a: Easy and Medium have unlimited moves. Hard allows 40 moves and Expert allows 50 moves per level.
sortstack.faq3a: Yes, the undo button is free and unlimited. Tap it as many times as you need.
sortstack.faq4a: Yes. Every puzzle in SortStack is generated by starting from a perfectly solved state and applying random reverse-moves. This guarantees that every layout is 100% solvable.

quickcalc.tagline: Race against time with mental math
quickcalc.metaDesc: QuickCalc is a speed math game. Solve problems against the clock. Can you handle the Operator Roulette?
quickcalc.guide: QuickCalc is a fast-paced mental math game. You start with 30 seconds on the clock. Each correct answer adds 2 seconds, but each wrong answer costs 3 seconds. Questions get progressively harder as you advance. Starting from question 15, Operator Roulette kicks in — instead of solving a calculation, you see an equation like '7 ? 3 = 21' and must figure out the missing operator. Build combos by answering correctly in a row for extra time bonuses. How high can you score before time runs out?
quickcalc.faq1a: From question 15, some problems show 'A ? B = C' where you must pick the correct operator (+, -, ×, ÷) instead of calculating an answer.
quickcalc.faq2q: How does the combo system work?
quickcalc.faq2a: Answer 3 in a row for +3 bonus seconds per correct answer. 5 in a row gives +4s, and 10+ gives +5s. Any wrong answer resets your combo.
quickcalc.faq3a: Yes, automatically. Early questions are simple single-digit addition. By question 20+, you'll face multi-digit multiplication and mixed operations.
quickcalc.faq4q: Are the wrong answers generated randomly?
quickcalc.faq4a: No! There's one near miss (±1-3), one medium miss (±5-15), and one far miss. Be careful — they're designed to trick you!

tileturn.tagline: Flip tiles to light them all
tileturn.metaDesc: TileTurn is a tile-flipping puzzle with 3 unique modes: Classic, Cascade, and Spectrum. Solve puzzles with logical thinking.
tileturn.guide: TileTurn is a logical tile-flipping puzzle. In Classic mode, tap a tile to toggle it and its four neighbors between ON and OFF. Your goal is to turn all tiles ON. Cascade mode adds a second layer — the toggle effect spreads two steps outward, affecting up to 13 tiles per tap. In Spectrum mode, tiles cycle through three colors instead of two. Tapping advances tiles to the next color. Your goal is to make all tiles the same color. Each level has a minimum number of taps to solve. Achieve that minimum for 3 stars!
tileturn.faq1a: The toggle effect spreads 2 levels deep. When you tap a tile, it and its 4 neighbors toggle (level 1), then THEIR neighbors also toggle (level 2). Up to 13 tiles can change from a single tap.
tileturn.faq2a: Instead of ON/OFF, tiles cycle through 3 colors: red → amber → blue → red. The goal is to make all tiles the same color. Much trickier than Classic!
tileturn.faq3a: Solve the puzzle in the minimum number of taps. 2 stars for minimum +1~2, and 1 star for anything more.
tileturn.faq4q: How does the Cascade mode work?
tileturn.faq4a: Cascade is a 2-step chain reaction. Step 1: tap toggles the target + adjacent tiles normally. After a 300ms delay, Step 2: tiles turned ON in Step 1 also toggle THEIR adjacent tiles. This creates ripple effects that require deeper planning.
tileturn.faq5a: In Spectrum mode, tiles cycle through 3 states: OFF (dark) → Blue → Green. Tapping advances the target and its neighbors by one state. The goal is to get ALL tiles to Green.

colorflow.tagline: Connect matching colors, fill the grid
colorflow.metaDesc: ColorFlow is a free path-connection puzzle. Link matching color pairs and fill every cell. Over 240 levels!
colorflow.guide: ColorFlow is a path-connection puzzle. Each grid has pairs of matching colored diamond markers. Draw paths to connect each pair by dragging horizontally or vertically. Paths cannot overlap. Fill every empty cell for a Perfect rating. The Flow Bonus rewards efficient paths — keep your path length within 1.5× the shortest possible distance for bonus points. Start with small 5×5 grids and work your way up to mind-bending 9×9 puzzles across 5 level packs.
colorflow.faq1a: Achieve 100% coverage — connect all pairs AND fill every empty cell with paths. 2 stars for 80%+ coverage, 1 star for just connecting all pairs.
colorflow.faq2a: If your path for a color pair is shorter than 1.5× the shortest possible route, you earn a bonus. Draw efficient paths!
colorflow.faq3a: Yes, tap on a connected marker to erase its path. You can also draw over existing paths to overwrite them.

pipelink.metaDesc: PipeLink is a circuit connection puzzle. Rotate tiles to connect the energy source to its destination. Features dual sources and locked tiles!
pipelink.guide: PipeLink is a circuit connection puzzle with a sleek circuit-board theme. Your job is to rotate tile pieces so that the energy source (⚡) connects to the destination (🔋) through a complete path. Tap any tile to rotate it 90° clockwise. Double-tap for 180°. From Pack 4, Dual Source puzzles introduce two separate paths that must both be connected simultaneously. From Pack 5, some tiles are locked and cannot be rotated — you must work around them. Connect all tiles for a Perfect rating!
pipelink.faq1a: Tap a tile once for 90° clockwise rotation. Double-tap for 180°. Locked tiles (🔒) cannot be rotated.
pipelink.faq2a: Starting from Pack 4, puzzles have 2 sources and 2 destinations (A and B). You must connect A→A and B→B simultaneously. Paths can share cross tiles.
pipelink.faq3a: From Pack 5, some tiles have a lock icon and cannot be rotated. You must solve the puzzle by rotating only the unlocked tiles.
pipelink.faq4a: Yes! Completing without hints earns a ×1.2 score multiplier. First hint is free, subsequent ones require a reward ad.

mergechain.metaDesc: MergeChain is a number-merging drop game. Combine matching balls to reach 2048! Simple physics, endless strategy.
mergechain.guide: MergeChain is a number-merging drop game. Position numbered balls along the top and release them. When two balls with the same number touch, they merge into the next number (2+2=4, 4+4=8, all the way to 2048). Chain reactions happen when a newly merged ball immediately touches another of the same number — plan your drops to set up chains for massive points. Watch the danger line at the top — if balls stack above it, the game is over. In Time Attack mode, you have 2 minutes to score as high as possible.
mergechain.faq1a: New balls are randomly 2, 4, or 8. Higher numbers only appear through merging.
mergechain.faq2a: If ball A+A merge into B, and that B touches an existing B, they immediately merge into C. The chain multiplier increases your score.
mergechain.faq3a: When any ball crosses the danger line at the top of the field.
mergechain.faq4a: The next ball to drop is always visible at the top of the screen. Dropped balls are randomly 2, 4, or 8, keeping the focus on merging them into larger numbers.
mergechain.faq5q: How does the Hint system work?
mergechain.faq5a: The 💡 Hint button shows a ghost ball at the optimal drop column. The first hint per session is free. Subsequent hints require watching a reward ad. Hints don't affect scoring.

hexmatch.tagline: Match hex tiles in chain reactions
hexmatch.metaDesc: HexMatch is a hexagonal color matching puzzle. Connect same-colored tiles, trigger hex bombs, and survive the rising tide!
hexmatch.guide: HexMatch is a hexagonal color matching game. Drag across adjacent tiles of the same color to connect 3 or more, then release to remove them. New tiles fall from above. Connect 5 or more to create a Hex Bomb — tap it on your next turn to explode all tiles within a 2-ring radius! Every 8 turns, the Rising Tide pushes a new row up from the bottom. If tiles reach the top, the game is over. Watch for Rainbow tiles that match any color. Build long chains and use bombs strategically to survive!
hexmatch.faq1a: Connect 5+ same-color tiles to create a bomb at the center. On your next turn, tap the bomb to destroy all tiles within 2 rings around it (up to 12 tiles).
hexmatch.faq2a: Every 8 turns, a new row of tiles pushes up from the bottom. This gradually fills the board. Clear tiles efficiently to survive!
hexmatch.faq3a: Rainbow tiles (⭐) match any color. They appear every 8 turns randomly and are very useful for making longer chains.
hexmatch.faq4a: The Rainbow tile (⭐) appears every 8 turns in a random position. It matches any color, so you can include it in any chain. Use it strategically to create longer chains or reach the 5-tile threshold for a Hex Bomb!
hexmatch.faq5a: Scoring depends on chain length: 3 tiles = 30 pts, 4 = 60, 5 = 120 (+ bomb!), 6 = 300, 7+ = 600. Bomb explosions earn 20 points per tile destroyed. Focus on longer chains and bombs for maximum score!
hexmatch.faq6q: How does the Hint system work?
hexmatch.faq6a: The 💡 Hint button highlights a valid 4+ chain with a golden outline for 2 seconds. The first hint per session is free; subsequent hints require watching a reward ad. If no matches exist, a Shuffle prompt appears instead.

pages.aboutContent: PuzzleVault is a collection of 10 free brain puzzle games that run entirely in your browser. No downloads. No accounts. No personal data collected. Just pure puzzle fun. Every game features daily challenges, score tracking, and unique mechanics you won't find anywhere else. Built with love using vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
pages.privacyIntro: Your privacy matters to us. Here's what you need to know:
pages.privacyNoData: We do not collect any personal data. No accounts, no sign-ups, no tracking.
pages.privacyLocalStorage: Game progress is saved locally in your browser using localStorage. This data never leaves your device.
pages.privacyAds: Google AdSense displays ads on this site and may use cookies. For details, see Google's privacy policy.
pages.privacyCookies: This site uses no first-party cookies. Third-party ad cookies are managed by Google.
pages.termsContent: PuzzleVault games are provided free of charge, as-is, with no warranties. The PuzzleVault name, logo, and original game designs are copyrighted. You're welcome to play and share your scores. Don't copy or redistribute our code or designs.
pages.contactIntro: Found a bug? Have a suggestion? We'd love to hear from you.
pages.contactThanks: Responses are reviewed regularly. Thank you for helping us improve!
